title: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? - Kuwait Times
summary: What does Kuwait’s OECD QDMTT recognition mean for multinationals? Kuwait Times
ai_summary: Kuwait has received OECD recognition for its Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT), signaling a significant step towards its Pillar Two implementation. This recognition impacts multinational enterprises operating in Kuwait, requiring them to factor Kuwait's QDMTT into their global minimum tax calculations and potentially reducing the top-up tax payable in other jurisdictions under the Income Inclusion Rule (IIR) or Undertaxed Profits Rule (UTPR).

title: Global Minimum Tax Creates Dispute Risks, Polish Official Says
summary: Global Minimum Tax Creates Dispute Risks, Polish Official Says news.bloombergtax.com

title: Proposed Directive to implement Pillar Two in the EU - KPMG
summary: Proposed Directive to implement Pillar Two in the EU KPMG

This directive aims to ensure consistent application of the GloBE rules, including the Income Inclusion Rule (IIR) and Undertaxed Payments Rule (UTPR), within the EU framework.

title: Portugal Must Reclaim Illegal State Aid, EU Court Says - Law360
summary: Portugal Must Reclaim Illegal State Aid, EU Court Says Law360

title: Finally, the European Court of Justice cracks down on trusts
summary: The Tax Justice Network has banged the drum about the threats posed by trusts for decades. On 21 May 2026, the European Court of Justice has finally joined us. They have published rulings that directly relate to trusts regarding two major risks: secrecy and asset protection. As we set out below, these represent a significant […]
ai_summary: The European Court of Justice issued significant rulings concerning trusts, directly addressing risks related to secrecy and asset protection. These judgments represent a landmark development in combating the misuse of trusts for tax avoidance and promoting greater transparency within the EU's financial systems.
